pattern: Order doesn't matter for the output, so this is a one-pass membership test per word: collect the index of every word that contains the target character at all.
hints: You only need a per-word yes/no answer -- does this word contain the character at least once anywhere?
Most languages give you a direct 'does this string contain this character' check; no need to scan character by character yourself.
Walk the words in order, and whenever a word contains the character, record its index -- the final order of the indices you collect doesn't matter.

edge_inputs.description: character only ever appears at the very end of qualifying words
character appears multiple times in the same word, still counts once

stress.description: random word list over a small alphabet with a random target letter
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    words = [''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 50))) for _ in range(n)]
    x = rng.choice('abcde')
    return [words, x]
